_synthetic_notice: Synthetic evidence map. Demonstrates the agent's output: for each payer question, a mapped answer + value message with inline citations, confidence, evidence-type tags, gap flags and localisation notes. In production this is generated by the AIProvider; here it is pre-computed for a deterministic demo.

entries.answer: In a head-to-head phase 3 trial, rimcazumab demonstrated superior skin clearance versus the class reference comparator: PASI 90 at week 16 was 71.8% versus 49.7%, a treatment difference of 22.1 percentage points (95% CI 15.3–28.9). Response was durable, maintained to week 52 in 88% of week-16 responders.
entries.value_message: Rimcazumab offers a clinically meaningful added benefit in complete-response skin clearance versus the appropriate comparator — the patient-relevant endpoint G-BA prioritises.

citations.quote: PASI 90 at week 16 was superior for rimcazumab versus ustekinumab (71.8% vs 49.7%; treatment difference 22.1 percentage points, 95% CI 15.3–28.9).
Response was maintained to week 52 in 88% of week-16 PASI 90 responders.

localisation.note: Confirm the comparator matches G-BA's defined zVT; the trial used the class reference, which aligns with common German practice.

entries.answer: Complete skin clearance (PASI 100) was achieved by 42.3% of patients at week 16, and IGA 0/1 by 78.1% versus 6.1% for placebo. Durability data show maintenance of high-level response through week 52.
entries.value_message: More than four in ten patients reach complete clearance — a high-bar, patient-relevant outcome that differentiates on depth of response.

citations.quote: PASI 100 (complete clearance) was reached by 42.3% of rimcazumab patients at week 16.
IGA 0/1 response was observed in 78.1% of rimcazumab patients versus 6.1% placebo at week 16.

entries.answer: Trial endpoints included DLQI 0/1 as a secondary measure, but a mapped DLQI 0/1 response rate for the target population was not located in the current corpus.
entries.value_message: Quality-of-life benefit is expected given the clearance depth, but the specific DLQI response rate must be sourced before this claim can be submitted.

gap.note: No DLQI 0/1 response rate found in indexed sources. Requires the LUMINA-1 secondary-endpoint appendix (not in demo corpus). Flag for evidence retrieval.

entries.answer: The most common adverse reactions (≥1%) were nasopharyngitis, upper respiratory tract infection and injection-site reactions. Serious infections occurred in 1.1% of patients over 52 weeks; latent tuberculosis evaluation is required prior to initiation.
entries.value_message: The safety profile is consistent with the IL-23 class and is manageable within routine dermatology monitoring, supporting a favourable benefit–risk balance.

citations.quote: The most common adverse reactions (≥1%) were nasopharyngitis, upper respiratory tract infection, and injection-site reactions.
Serious infections were reported in 1.1% of treated patients over 52 weeks; evaluate for latent tuberculosis prior to initiation.

entries.answer: In a 24-month real-world registry, 66.9% of patients achieved absolute PASI ≤2 by month 6 — consistent with trial estimates — and 24-month persistence was 79.4% versus a registry biologic average of 64.2%.
entries.value_message: Trial efficacy translates to routine practice with above-average persistence, reducing the risk of therapy switching and its associated costs.

citations.quote: In routine practice, 66.9% of patients achieved absolute PASI ≤2 by month 6, consistent with trial-based estimates.
24-month treatment persistence was 79.4%, higher than the registry biologic average of 64.2%.

localisation.note: Registry is pan-European; confirm German subgroup is representative for IQWiG.

entries.answer: Plaque psoriasis affects ~2.0% of German adults, ~25% with moderate-to-severe disease. Mean annual direct cost per moderate-to-severe patient is ~€7,180 plus ~€3,540 indirect costs. Around 30% of biologic-eligible patients do not achieve sustained clear skin on first-line systemic therapy.
entries.value_message: A substantial, high-cost population with clear unmet need — rimcazumab's complete-clearance rate directly addresses the first-line failure gap.

citations.quote: Plaque psoriasis affects approximately 2.0% of the German adult population; an estimated 25% have moderate-to-severe disease.
Mean annual direct cost per moderate-to-severe patient was estimated at €7,180, with indirect (productivity) costs adding a further €3,540.
Approximately 30% of biologic-eligible patients do not achieve sustained clear/almost-clear skin on first-line systemic therapy.

localisation.note: German-specific burden data — directly usable for G-BA.

entries.answer: Maintenance dosing is every 12 weeks (after weeks 0 and 4). In real-world data this schedule was associated with a 23% lower missed-dose rate versus every-8-week comparators.
entries.value_message: A quarterly maintenance schedule lowers administration burden and improves adherence — supporting durable outcomes and reduced healthcare contact.

citations.quote: 150 mg administered by subcutaneous injection at weeks 0 and 4, followed by every 12 weeks thereafter.
The every-12-week maintenance schedule was associated with a 23% lower missed-dose rate versus every-8-week comparators.

entries.answer: A 3-year budget-impact estimate versus the comparator could not be produced from the current corpus, which contains no pricing or net-cost inputs.
entries.value_message: Budget impact requires the German net price and a comparator cost basket — to be supplied by the market-access economics team before submission.

gap.note: No pricing/cost-offset data in the open corpus. Requires internal pricing model (post-NDA). Correctly flagged rather than estimated.

localisation.note: Budget impact is inherently market-specific; must be built on German list/net price.
